findings.description: The file opens with a corrected supersession note citing ACS B07001_081E and ~20.5x ratio, but the body retains the old method (ACS B05005), old row count (9,120), and old 33x claim.
findings.fix: Split the newcomer memo into a current version and an archival provenance appendix to remove live semantic contradictions.
findings.title: Archival newcomer memo is internally contradictory

findings.description: Confidence ladder item 22 and the index cite the newcomer memo for downgraded claims, but the cited source still contains old 33x methodology and results in its body, breaking traceability.
findings.fix: Update the citation graph to point only to updated, consistent content or separate the current findings into a new file.
findings.title: Current citation graph points to semantically stale source

findings.description: Function fetch_acs_county_population_and_recent_fb() docstring describes old estimand (B05005_002E + B05005_004E) while the code actually requests a different set of variables (B01003, B05002, etc.).
findings.fix: Update function docstrings and code comments to match the actual variable definitions and requests.
findings.title: Newcomer script semantics were only partially cleaned

findings.description: README states outputs land in data/analysis/, but the actual falsification tranche artifacts are located under data/outcomes/analysis/.
findings.fix: Correct the README to reflect the actual artifact locations in data/outcomes/analysis/.
findings.title: README output-path contract is stale

findings.description: The design uses only 60 actual and 60 null splits. The memo uses qualitative descriptors like 'beats the null strongly' instead of exact Monte Carlo precision or actual-vs-null differences.
findings.fix: Increase THRESHOLD_SPLITS and export exact uncertainty metrics like actual-null mean difference and split-seed SD.
findings.title: Threshold-search confidence is qualitatively downgraded but under-quantified

findings.description: The supersession note gives 4.49% and 0.21%, and states 'roughly 20.5x', but 4.49 / 0.21 equals 21.38.
findings.fix: State whether the ratio uses rounded or exact medians to resolve the calculation discrepancy.
findings.title: Arithmetic ambiguity in newcomer ratio presentation

findings.description: There is a lack of metadata (universe, time window, unit) for analysis artifacts, leading to potential semantic drift between non-equivalent constructs like IRS and ACS migration data.
findings.fix: Attach a measurement contract JSON to each output dataset with fields for universe, time_window, unit, and comparability_warning.
findings.title: Missing machine-readable measurement metadata

findings.description: The newcomer comparison is currently descriptive but noisy because it lacks filtering for unreliable counties based on ACS Margin of Error (MOE).
findings.fix: Fetch ACS MOEs for B07001_081E and filter/flag counties with unreliable estimates.
findings.title: Missing ACS MOE filtering for newcomer comparison

findings.description: The repository lacks an automated check to prevent stale-number regressions where memo text drifts from the actual data in parquet/CSV summaries.
findings.fix: Implement a linter that validates cited numbers in memos against the current output artifacts.
findings.title: Missing memo-artifact consistency linter

findings.description: Major files contain unsourced narrative blocks and stale internals, specifically in the newcomer memo where the header/body contradiction breaks traceability.
findings.fix: Inline-source quantitative claims directly from the data artifacts and split archival notes from current content.
findings.title: Failure to source internal narrative blocks

findings.description: Claims like 'beats null strongly' and 'policy relevance high' are not sufficiently quantified with exact effect-size deltas or uncertainty intervals.
findings.fix: Add exact effect-size deltas and uncertainty intervals to qualitative claims.
findings.title: Under-quantification of evidence strength
